activities_projects.title: Cross Multiplication Investigation
steps: Write pairs of ratios such as 3:4 and 9:12
Use cross multiplication to check if they are proportional
Explain why the ratios are proportional or not
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies cross multiplication to test proportions

activities_projects.title: Scale Drawing Project
steps: Draw a rectangle of size 4 cm by 3 cm
Draw another rectangle with twice the dimensions
Compare the ratios of the sides

activities_projects.title: Equivalent Ratio Exploration
steps: Choose a ratio like 5:7
Generate five equivalent ratios
Explain how they are related

activities_projects.title: Recipe Scaling Challenge
steps: Write ingredients for a recipe serving 4 people
Scale the recipe for 10 people
Explain the ratio used

activities_projects.title: Distance–Time Ratio Activity
steps: Assume a car travels 60 km in 1 hour
Find the distance for 2, 3 and 4 hours
Explain how the quantities are proportional

activities_projects.title: Missing Term in Proportion
steps: Write proportions like 7:9 :: x:27
Find the value of x using cross multiplication
Verify the result

activities_projects.title: Graph of Proportional Values
steps: Write proportional pairs such as (2,4), (4,8), (6,12)
Plot them on graph paper
Observe the pattern formed

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Ratio Investigation
steps: Collect data such as number of boys and girls in class
Write the ratio
Compare ratios with another class

activities_projects.title: Proportional Rectangle Investigation
steps: Draw rectangles with ratios 3:2 such as 6×4 and 9×6
Measure the sides
Explain why they are proportional

activities_projects.title: Ratio Research Poster
steps: Research examples of ratios in real life
Create a poster showing at least three examples
Explain the ratios involved
